title: GetResponse vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse and Drip email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which tool suits your business.
h1: GetResponse vs Drip: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: GetResponse and Drip are both powerful email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. GetResponse is a versatile all-in-one marketing suite ideal for course creators and webinar hosts, while Drip specializes in e-commerce email marketing with deep store integration. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your business goals.

comparison_table.tool_a: Free plan (up to 500 subscribers), paid plans from $19/month
comparison_table.tool_b: No free plan, paid plans start at $39/month

comparison_table.tool_a: Course creators, webinar hosts, digital marketers
comparison_table.tool_b: E-commerce businesses, online stores, product-focused brands

comparison_table.tool_a: Email, webinars, landing pages, automation, courses, funnels
comparison_table.tool_b: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, SMS, visual automation, onsite campaigns

choose_a_if: You want to start with a free plan and scale gradually as your business grows
You need webinars, landing pages, and online course hosting built into one platform
You're creating digital products, courses, or hosting webinars alongside email marketing
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce business and need deep integration with your online store
You want revenue attribution and advanced e-commerce tracking capabilities
Your primary focus is email marketing optimization for product sales

faq.a: It depends on your needs. GetResponse is superior for businesses requiring webinars, courses, and multi-channel marketing, while Drip excels for e-commerce companies needing specialized store integration and revenue tracking. GetResponse has a higher overall feature set, while Drip achieves a slightly higher G2 rating (4.4 vs 4.2).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Drip?
faq.a: GetResponse is significantly cheaper to start, offering a free plan for up to 500 subscribers with no credit card required. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. However, GetResponse's paid plans begin at $19/month, making it the more budget-friendly option overall.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list imports and exports, making migration relatively straightforward. You'll need to export your contacts from Drip and import them into GetResponse, though automation workflows and campaign settings will need to be recreated manually in most cases.
